codeReview.summary: Fail. Commit `147679b` added a substantial hosted-provider mount payload layer and the handoff/transcript show the intended targeted test plus workspace validation were run. However, the current GCS mapping for the rclone-backed providers (E2B, Daytona, Runloop) does not preserve the upstream auth-mode semantics: it forwards HMAC and native GCS auth fields together instead of choosing one mode or rejecting the combination, so some invalid/ambiguous inputs do not fail clearly.

issues.description: `build_rclone_gcs_payload()` always copies every GCS auth field into the payload: HMAC credentials (`access_id`/`secret_access_key`) become `credentials`, while `service_account_credentials` and `access_token` are also forwarded unchanged. For E2B/Daytona/Runloop this means a mount that supplies both HMAC and native GCS auth produces one payload containing both auth modes at once, instead of following the upstream split behavior (HMAC uses the S3-compatible path; native auth uses the GCS path) or rejecting the unsupported combination. That violates the feature requirement that unsupported auth combinations fail clearly and means the generated payload is not a faithful provider-native config for mixed-auth GCS mounts.

The mount-strategy regression test exercises a GCS access-token case for Daytona and basic happy-path/error cases, but it never covers mixed-auth GCS inputs for the rclone-backed providers. Because there is no case with both HMAC and native GCS auth present, the ambiguous payload behavior above can slip through while the suite still passes.

summary: Reviewed the feature record, mission docs, AGENTS/services/architecture guidance, the worker handoff, the transcript skeleton, commit `147679b`, and the current hosted mount implementation/tests. The feature landed in `147679b`, but the current rclone-backed GCS mount mapping still accepts ambiguous mixed-auth input instead of normalizing or rejecting it, so I marked the review as fail with one blocking implementation issue and one related test-gap note.
